disability.summary: ED with primary anxiety presentation (generalized + performance/test anxiety). Academic skills are at grade level when Devon is regulated. Anxiety triggers — timed assessments, cold-calling, oral presentations, unannounced changes — produce somatic symptoms (stomachache, freeze, tearfulness) and lead to escape behaviors. A current Functional Behavior Assessment (FBA) and Behavior Intervention Plan (BIP) are in place; alternate test setting is required.

this_year.ela: Write a real essay without throwing it away first.
this_year.math: Finish my work on the days I'm doing okay.
this_year.social: Stop crying in front of the class.
this_year.tests: Take tests in Ms. R's room, not the big classroom.

long_term: Get to high school without missing a whole quarter.
Tell an adult I'm panicking before I run out of the room.

academics_general.current_performance: On grade level across content areas when work is completed. iReady math: G7 (Fall 2025). iReady reading: G7. Work-completion rate is the bottleneck — 45% of in-class assignments turned in over the last marking period; 90% of attempted work is at grade-level proficiency. Strong written work when given choice of topic and quiet setting.
academics_general.strengths: Thoughtful, articulate in writing, kind to younger students, strong reader, deep interest in history (WWII, civil rights movement) and graphic novels.
academics_general.impact: Anxiety drives avoidance: incomplete classwork, refusal to attempt timed tasks, escape from room when called on. Without alternate setting, co-regulation script, and FBA-aligned breaks, Devon's measured performance dramatically underestimates his ability.

ela.current_performance: Reading on grade level. Written expression on grade level when not under time/performance pressure — produces multi-paragraph essays with clear thesis and evidence when working alone in resource room. In whole-group setting, often submits blank paper or single sentence. Strongly prefers writing over speaking.
ela.strengths: Clear voice, strong organization, willingness to revise when feedback is delivered privately in writing.
ela.homework_independent_accuracy: 70–85% when completed at home; ~30% completion rate for in-class writing
ela.with_alternate_setting_accuracy: 85–95% on the same prompts completed in resource room

math.current_performance: On grade level conceptually. Work-completion stamina is the goal area: starts assignments but stops at first error or unfamiliar problem type, often crumples paper or asks to leave.
math.current_average: 62% (driven by incomplete work; attempted-work accuracy is 88%)

behavioral_social_emotional.current_performance: Diagnosed with generalized anxiety and specific performance/test anxiety. FBA (Sept 2025) identified triggers: timed tasks, cold-calling, oral presentation, unexpected schedule changes, conflict with peers. Function of escape behavior: avoidance of perceived performance failure. BIP includes: co-regulation script with case manager, warning-signs sheet (laminated, kept in folder), 5-minute breaks on request without explanation required, 'silent signal' (red card on desk) to leave the room without verbal exchange. Sees outside therapist weekly; school counselor 2x/week.
behavioral_social_emotional.strengths: Self-aware, articulate about his anxiety in 1:1 settings, engages well with trusted adults, recovers quickly when given space.
behavioral_social_emotional.impact: Untreated anxiety episodes can cost 30–90 minutes of instructional time and produce lasting reluctance to re-enter the triggering setting. With BIP in place, average recovery time is 8 minutes.
behavioral_social_emotional.bullying_vulnerability: Elevated due to visible distress responses; self-advocacy and confidentiality of break system are protective.

presentation_of_instruction: No cold-calling — Devon raises hand or signals to participate
Directions given in writing whenever oral directions are given
Advance written notice of any oral presentation (minimum 5 school days) with option to record video alternative
Co-regulation script reviewed with case manager at start of each day
response: Written response accepted in place of any oral response
Typed response accepted for any handwritten task
Option to submit work via shared doc rather than hand in physically
Errors not marked in red ink
timing_scheduling: 5-minute breaks on request, no explanation required, up to 4 per day
Assignment chunking: long tasks broken into segments with check-in after each segment
No same-day assessments after a documented anxiety episode
setting_environment: Alternate setting (resource room with case manager) REQUIRED for all formal assessments
Seat near door for low-profile exit
Red-card 'silent signal' on desk; teacher acknowledges with nod, no verbal exchange
Warning-signs sheet (laminated) accessible in folder at all times

modifications.instruction: BIP-aligned: never require oral performance in front of whole class without 5-day notice and recorded-video alternative. Assessments administered in alternate setting only. Work-completion measured against attempted-work accuracy, not raw completion rate, until stamina goal is met.

annual_goals.baseline: Recognizes panic onset after the fact in 2/5 opportunities; uses a coping strategy (box breathing, grounding 5-4-3-2-1, red-card break) before escalation in 1/5 opportunities. Average recovery time post-episode: 8 min with BIP, 35+ min without.
annual_goals.goal: Independently identify an early warning sign from the personal warning-signs sheet and deploy a taught coping strategy (box breathing, 5-4-3-2-1 grounding, red-card break, request co-regulation) BEFORE escalation in 4 of 5 opportunities, with average post-episode recovery under 10 minutes.
annual_goals.method: BIP data sheet, teacher observation, counselor log, self-report check-in
benchmarks: Read warning-signs sheet aloud with case manager at morning check-in 4/5 days.
Use red-card silent signal independently when warning signs appear, 4/5 opps.
Complete a 60-second box-breathing cycle before re-entering classroom, 4/5 opps.
Self-rate anxiety 1–10 at end of day on tracking log, 4/5 days.
Re-enter triggering setting within 10 minutes of leaving, 4/5 opps.

annual_goals.baseline: Produces grade-level multi-paragraph essays in alternate setting (85–95% rubric score). In whole-group setting, completes ~30% of writing tasks; submitted work averages 1.8 on standards-based scale.
annual_goals.goal: Independently complete and submit grade-level written responses (multi-paragraph essay, short constructed response, journal entry) at a 2.5+ rubric score in 4 of 5 assignments per quarter, with at least 3 of those completed in the general education setting (rather than alternate setting).
annual_goals.criteria: 80% of assignments at 2.5+ rubric, with progressive shift to gen ed setting
annual_goals.method: Work samples scored against grade-level rubric, completion log
benchmarks: Use a written planning template (claim → 3 evidence → conclusion) before drafting, 4/5 assignments.
Complete first paragraph in gen ed setting before optional move to alternate setting, 3/5 assignments.
Submit final draft (typed) within agreed extended deadline, 4/5 assignments.
Respond in writing to teacher feedback within 48 hours, 4/5 instances.

annual_goals.baseline: Attempts ~45% of in-class math assignments. Of attempted work, 88% accuracy. Stops at first error or unfamiliar problem; often escapes the room.
annual_goals.goal: Increase in-class math work completion from 45% to 80% over the IEP year, while maintaining attempted-work accuracy at 80%+ — using assignment chunking, the red-card break, and the 'skip-and-return' protocol.
annual_goals.criteria: 80% completion across 4 consecutive weeks, 80% accuracy maintained
annual_goals.owner: SE Classroom Teacher / Gen Ed Math Teacher
benchmarks: Complete one assignment chunk before requesting a break, 4/5 opportunities.
Use 'skip-and-return' protocol on unfamiliar problems instead of stopping, 4/5 opportunities.
Submit work even when incomplete, marking unfinished items with a star rather than discarding, 4/5 assignments.
Request teacher check-in (in writing) after each assignment chunk, 4/5 chunks.

placement: Full Inclusion Program with alternate setting for assessments (services outside gen ed < 21% of time; assessment time accounts for the majority of pull-out)

mcas: Separate or alternate test location — REQUIRED, not optional (DF4)
Test administration over multiple days for long sessions (T2)
